title: Permit Exploration CRs under existing scope-integrity machinery

comment:
Pre-review of CR-115 v0.1 (IN_PRE_REVIEW).

VERIFIED COMPLIANT:
- Frontmatter title: 'Permit Exploration CRs under existing scope-integrity machinery' (present, non-empty).
- Frontmatter revision_summary: present and substantive, documenting two Lead amendments with section-level traceability.
- Document structure: All SOP-002 Section 6 required sections present (1-12), with appropriate subsections (2.1-2.3, 5.1-5.7, 7.1-7.3, 9.1-9.6).
- All cross-references verified valid: QMS-Policy Section 6 (Scope Integrity, line 112), SOP-002 Section 6.2 and 7.1, SOP-002 Section 7.3, SOP-004 Section 9A (line 209), SOP-005 Section 7.1.3, SOP-006 Section 6.5 (line 209), SOP-006 Section 7.4, scope-change-guide.md, qa.md, CR-113, CR-114.
- Design collaboration record exists at .claude/sessions/Session-2026-05-03-001/exploration-cr-design.md (verified file present).
- Files Affected list (Section 2.3) and Impact Assessment (Section 7.1, 7.2) are mutually consistent.
- Reviewer assignment: per qa.md guidance, Quality Manual / SOP / agent-definition revisions do not require TU assignment when no Flow State domain review surface is affected. QA-only routing is correct.

DEFICIENCIES REQUIRING UPDATES:

1. **Literal 'TBD' string in Section 9.4 Phase 4 step 1.** The text reads: 'Numbering and placement TBD at execution based on current qa.md structure.' Per QA review criteria, TBD is a placeholder and grounds for rejection. Replace with a definite statement, e.g.: 'Numbering and placement to be selected at execution time based on the qa.md structure at HEAD; recommended placement is a new subsection appended after the current 'Reviewer Assignment for Pipe Dream' section, titled 'Exploration CRs'.'

2. **Deferred-design language in Section 5.4 (Change Description).** Section 5.4 opens with 'Add a new subsection (numbering to be assigned at execution time based on current qa.md structure)'. Section 5 is pre-approved content — a reviewer cannot evaluate the placement of a new subsection if its position in the file is left to execution-time judgment. Specify the intended placement (e.g., 'append a new top-level section titled "Exploration CRs" after the existing "Reviewer Assignment for Pipe Dream" section'). The numbering itself can remain implicit since the file uses prose section headers, not numbered sections — that is a genuine non-issue, but the placement is not.

3. **Cross-document consistency claim in Section 8 Testing Summary references 'a/b/c' lowercase.** Section 8 bullet 5 says 'qa.md (a/b/c)'. Section 5.4 uses '(a)', '(b)', '(c)' — consistent with §8. No deficiency on naming, but flag: when execution lands these items in qa.md, the chosen numbering scheme (literal a/b/c labels vs numbered list) should be locked here so post-review can verify the labels match. Recommend Section 5.4 explicitly state the items will be labeled '(a)', '(b)', '(c)' in the final qa.md text (this is currently implicit).

The two TBD/deferred-placement issues (items 1 and 2) are blocking. Item 3 is a recommendation to tighten pre-approved specificity but is not on its own a blocking deficiency — addressing it strengthens the document.

Please revise and re-route for pre-review.

PRE-REVIEW PASS — CR-115 v0.1 'Permit Exploration CRs under existing scope-integrity machinery'.

FRONTMATTER VERIFICATION:
- title: 'Permit Exploration CRs under existing scope-integrity machinery' — present, non-empty, matches sidecar.
- revision_summary: present and descriptive (technically N/A for v0.x but provided; documents the prior QA cycle 1 corrections — placement of qa.md insert, removal of 'subsection numbering TBD' language in §5.4 / §9.4 / EI-9, locked (a)/(b)/(c) labels as immutable identifiers, and the Lead amendments on TU REQ-candidacy advisory and out-of-bound VAR handling).

DOCUMENT STRUCTURE (per Quality-Manual/types/CR.md):
All 12 top-level sections present in correct order: Purpose, Scope, Current State, Proposed State, Change Description, Justification, Impact Assessment, Testing Summary, Implementation Plan, Execution, Execution Summary, References. §2 has required subsections 2.1 Context (with Parent Document: None), 2.2 Changes Summary, 2.3 Files Affected. §5 numbered 5.1–5.7. §7 has 7.1, 7.2, 7.3 — §7.4 Development Controls and §7.5 Qualified State Continuity correctly omitted as this is a document-only CR (no controlled code modified). §9 has 9.1–9.6 with document-only phasing — code-CR phases (Test Env, Qualification, Integration Verification, RTM Update, Merge and Submodule) correctly omitted.

PLACEHOLDER CONTENT:
No author-time {{...}} placeholders remain. The [SQUARE_BRACKETS] in §10 EI table rows ([SUMMARY], [Pass/Fail], [PERFORMER], [DATE]), Execution Comments ([COMMENT]), and §11 ([EXECUTION_SUMMARY]) are the standard execution-time placeholders per CR-type reference (these remain until execution and are correct). The two 'TBD' string occurrences are inside the revision_summary frontmatter, where they describe what was *previously* TBD and has now been fixed (cycle 1 narrative) — they are not unresolved placeholders in document body.

TRACEABILITY:
- CR-113 (referenced as Quality-Manual submodule precedent + qa.md §5.3 SOP/QM-revision-no-TU rule): exists at QMS/CR/CR-113/CR-113.md.
- CR-114 (referenced as cost-incident motivator): exists at QMS/CR/CR-114/CR-114.md (CLOSED 2026-05-02 — verified against recent commit history).
- Design collaboration record: .claude/sessions/Session-2026-05-03-001/exploration-cr-design.md — exists.
- SOP-002 §6.2 (line 99 'Scope'), §7.1 (line 181 'Review Team Assignment'), §7.3 (line 202 'Post-Review Requirements') — all exist as referenced.
- SOP-004 §9A 'Variance Reports (VAR)' (line 209) — exists as referenced.
- SOP-005 §7.1.3 'Merge Gate and Merge Type' (line 185) — exists as referenced.
- SOP-006 §6 'Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM)' (line 118), §6.5 'RTM Maintenance' (line 209), §7.4 'CR Closure Prerequisite' (line 260) — all exist as referenced.
- QMS-Policy §6 'Scope Integrity' (line 112) — exists as referenced.
- qa.md insertion target: §5.4 specifies insertion 'between lines 138 and 140 of the current qa.md, immediately before the --- separator that precedes Review Criteria.' Verified: line 138 is the closing 'For SDLC documents…handbook.md' paragraph of Reviewer Assignment for Pipe Dream; line 140 is the --- separator; line 142 begins ## Review Criteria. Placement instruction is unambiguous and accurate to current file state.
- Quality-Manual/QMS-Policy.md, Quality-Manual/guides/scope-change-guide.md, QMS/SOP/SOP-002.md, .claude/agents/qa.md all exist.

CONTENT CONSISTENCY:
- §1 correctly identifies CR-115 itself as a Standard CR (not exploratory) — appropriate, since this is a deliberate process change with foreseeable consequences.
- §2.3 Files Affected, §7.1 Files Affected, §9 Implementation Plan, §10 EI table all enumerate the same four files with consistent treatment.
- §5.4 qa.md additions and §5.5 scope-change-guide.md note are mutually consistent on out-of-bound work being handled via VAR (per SOP-004 §9A) and that the VAR's resolution may authorize a new CR for legitimate scope expansion (the VAR itself does not expand the bound).
- §5.6 Trade-offs accepted lists four trade-offs; §7.3 Other Impacts cross-references back to §5.6 and §5.7. Consistent.
- §5.7 Rollback criterion: 'first three Exploration CRs' probation period with judgment-based, Lead-invoked criteria is concrete enough to be actionable.
- (a)/(b)/(c) labels in §5.4 are explicitly declared immutable identifiers; post-review verification path is named.
- TU REQ-candidacy as advisory only (qa.md (c)): consistent across §4, §5.4, §5.6.
- EI table (10 EIs) maps cleanly to the 5 phases described in §9.

NO TYPOS OR FORMATTING ERRORS FOUND. Markdown structure consistent throughout.

QA DISCRETION ON TU PRE-REVIEW: This CR modifies QMS-Policy, SOP-002, qa.md, and a Quality-Manual guide. Per the existing CR-113 precedent (referenced in §12), SOP/QM revisions do not require TU review at pre-approval. No project TU is required. No code is modified. Proceeding to pre-approval routing without TU assignment is appropriate.

RECOMMENDATION: RECOMMEND. Document is ready for pre-approval routing.

Post-review of CR-115 v1.1 finds a scope-integrity deviation in Execution Comment 2's verbatim sourcing claim, requiring update before recommendation.

VERIFIED COMPLIANT:
- Frontmatter title: 'Permit Exploration CRs under existing scope-integrity machinery' (present, descriptive)
- Frontmatter revision_summary: Present, descriptive of execution recording (10 EIs, no VARs, final commits cited)
- Document structure: 12 sections per TEMPLATE-CR; §10 EI table populated for all 10 EIs; §11 Execution Summary authored
- Every EI has Pass outcome with traceable evidence (commit hashes, document versions, PR number)
- pipe-dream HEAD = 9e61653 matches EI-10 claim
- Quality-Manual submodule pointer = c6a0a04 matches EI-6 claim; PR #2 merge commit verified
- Both QM qualified commits a07b990 (QMS-Policy §6) and e666d72 (scope-change-guide) are ancestors of c6a0a04 (verified via git merge-base --is-ancestor)
- SOP-002 v17.0 EFFECTIVE matches EI-8 claim
- qa.md ## Exploration CRs section at line 142, between ## Reviewer Assignment and ## Review Criteria, byte-equivalent to §5.4 verbatim with immutable (a)/(b)/(c) labels intact
- QMS-Policy §6 paragraph at QM@a07b990 byte-equivalent to §5.1 verbatim
- No VARs required (no failed EIs, no scope expansion within the scope as committed)

FINDING — Execution Comment 2's verbatim sourcing claim is contradicted by two of the five document additions:

1. SOP-002 §6.2 Exploratory CRs note. CR-115 §5.2 verbatim text reads:
   '...identified as "Exploratory" in §1 Purpose), the Scope section MUST include...'
   Actual EFFECTIVE text in SOP-002 v17.0 reads:
   '...identified as "Exploratory" in §1 Purpose **per QMS-Policy §6**), the Scope section MUST include...'
   The phrase 'per QMS-Policy §6' is not in the §5.2 verbatim block.

2. scope-change-guide.md note (QM@e666d72). CR-115 §5.5 verbatim text reads:
   'When execution in an Exploration CR encounters work that would exit the bounds...'
   Actual EFFECTIVE text reads:
   'When execution in an Exploration CR **(per QMS-Policy §6 + SOP-002 §6.2)** encounters work that would exit the bounds...'
   The parenthetical cross-reference is not in the §5.5 verbatim block.

Both additions are substantively benign cross-reference enhancements that arguably improve cross-document linkage. However, Execution Comment 2 explicitly claims:
'Verbatim sourcing convention applied throughout... cross-document consistency check at post-review will verify byte-equivalent text in the EFFECTIVE artifacts.'

Per QMS-Policy §6, scope-integrity is the post-review check the executor invited. The deviations are real (non-byte-equivalent) and were not handled via VAR. This is the gap between pre-approved plan and execution that the pre/post split exists to surface.

REMEDIATION OPTIONS (executor's choice):

Option A — Honest reframing (preferred, no code changes):
Revise Execution Comment 2 to acknowledge that two of the five additions deviated from strict byte-equivalence by adding inline cross-references ('per QMS-Policy §6' in SOP-002 §6.2; '(per QMS-Policy §6 + SOP-002 §6.2)' in scope-change-guide). State the rationale (cross-reference enhancement, no semantic change). State why this did not warrant a VAR (purely additive cross-document linkage; no behavioral or interpretive impact on the rule). The §11 Execution Summary may need a parallel honest note.

Option B — Strict revert (heavyweight, not recommended):
File a VAR documenting the deviation; revert SOP-002 to v18.0 with byte-equivalent §5.2 text; cut a new Quality-Manual execution branch + PR to revert the scope-change-guide cross-reference. Cost is disproportionate to the benefit.

Option C — Lead authorization for after-the-fact scope expansion:
Document a deliberate decision by the Lead that minor cross-reference enhancements during verbatim insertion are acceptable; amend Execution Comment 2 accordingly; add a process note for future verbatim-sourcing CRs.

Option A satisfies the scope-integrity check (the deviation is named and reasoned, not silent) at minimal cost. The QMS principle is that deviations must be documented, not that they must be reverted.

OUT OF SCOPE FOR THIS CYCLE: §11 cross-document consistency self-check correctly identified the SOP-002 §6.2 / qa.md (a) / scope-change-guide triple as mutually consistent — that observation stands. The cross-reference enhancements actually strengthen the consistency, not weaken it. The finding here is procedural (verbatim claim was inaccurate), not substantive (the rule content is sound).

Post-review cycle 2 of CR-115 v1.1 finds full compliance. All cycle 1 findings remediated.

Cycle 1 remediation verified (Option A — honest reframing applied):
- Execution Comment 2 now acknowledges the SOP-002 §6.2 added 'per QMS-Policy §6' inline cross-reference (not in §5.2 verbatim)
- Execution Comment 2 now acknowledges the scope-change-guide.md added '(per QMS-Policy §6 + SOP-002 §6.2)' inline cross-reference (not in §5.5 verbatim)

- Comment clearly identifies that the other three additions (QMS-Policy §6 paragraph, SOP-002 §7.1 note, qa.md ## Exploration CRs section) ARE byte-equivalent

Other:

RECOMMEND for QA approval.
